raw_consumer_statement:
Transaction Date : XX/XX/year> Merchant : XXXX Amount : {$710.00} Amex Offer : Spend $ XXXX, Get {$120.00} Back Disputed Amount : {$120.00} Statement Credit Prior to the transaction, I properly added the " XXXX : Spend $ XXXX, Get {$120.00} Back '' offer to my American Express card ending in 11005. 
Before making the purchase, I utilized the " View Store Locator '' link provided directly on the Amex Offer page to ensure the location I was visiting was a participating merchant. The specific store I visited was explicitly listed as an eligible location in the official American Express store locator for this promotion. 
On XX/XX/year>, I completed a purchase of {$710.00} at this verified location. 
Despite spending over the {$600.00} threshold at a verified location, the {$120.00} statement credit was never applied to my account. 
I contacted American Express customer service, and they denied the credit, stating that " outlet locations are excluded. '' However, I pointed out that this specific location was explicitly included in their own promotional locator tool. 

American Express engaged in deceptive advertising by providing a " Store Locator '' tool that explicitly listed this location as eligible. As a consumer, I relied on the information provided within the promotional interface to make a significant financial decision ( {$710.00} ). The presence of a general exclusion in the fine print does not negate the specific inclusion provided by the merchant locator tool, which serves as the primary guidance for consumers.
